claim: Sycophancy—AI models agreeing with and reinforcing opinions too much—is the biggest problem in coding agents; the worst case is an agent evaluating its own work
actionable: Never have a single agent both implement and evaluate code; use a separate adversarial agent to critically review implementation
source_title: Coding Agent Reliability EXPLODES When They Argue

claim: Multi-agent harnesses using GAN-inspired evaluation (generator vs. discriminator agents) significantly improve agent reliability for complex tasks
actionable: Use a three-agent architecture (planner, generator/implementer, evaluator) where the evaluator has its own context and actively challenges the generator's work
source_title: Coding Agent Reliability EXPLODES When They Argue

claim: Generator and evaluator agents should negotiate a contract upfront—agree on phases, evaluation criteria, and success thresholds before implementation
actionable: Before the implementer (generator) starts coding, have it and the evaluator agree on sprint boundaries, feature scope, and evaluation criteria (1-10 scoring per criterion)
source_title: Coding Agent Reliability EXPLODES When They Argue

claim: A single-agent harness built a non-functional retro game; the GAN-inspired three-agent harness built a fully playable version with elaborate sprite editor
actionable: Compare single-agent vs. multi-agent harness results on your own codebase to measure the reliability improvement
source_title: Coding Agent Reliability EXPLODES When They Argue

claim: Multi-agent harnesses enable cheaper models (e.g., Claude Sonnet) to produce results previously requiring more expensive models (e.g., Opus)
actionable: Use Claude Sonnet with a three-agent harness instead of upgrading to Opus; the harness compensates for the model downgrade
source_title: Coding Agent Reliability EXPLODES When They Argue

claim: Token cost goes up in multi-agent harnesses but is worth it for reliability; one-shot completion is possible where single-agent agents would require multiple iterations
actionable: Budget for higher token usage with multi-agent harnesses; measure total cost (tokens + human time) rather than tokens alone
source_title: Coding Agent Reliability EXPLODES When They Argue
